title: Date filter on the time tracking column
excerpt: My team is complaining that since moving to monday.com, they can no longer put a date filter on their time tracking entries to see how much time they have booked to a particular project that day. We very much need a mean…

posts.author: StephRH (Steph King)
posts.cookedHtml:
My team is complaining that since moving to monday.com, they can no longer put a date filter on their time tracking entries to see how much time they have booked to a particular project that day. We very much need a means to filter individual time tracking entries by day, not just the limited total time filters currently available.

The time tracking widget can break down time entries to the day at group level, so clearly some of this functionality is possible. If the time tracking widget could show the individual item values within the groups as well as the group total, this would do it.

posts.author: tibor93 (Tibor Tasi)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @StephRH,

One solution would be if each team members create a separate sub task to that item and track their time separately.

My other idea would be to check out the app marketplace. There are several time tracking app because the built-in column has some limitations.

Regards,
Tibor

posts.author: StephRH (Steph King)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks @tibor93. Unfortunately the first one doesn’t work for us because our team would still have to spend time trawling around their time boards looking at individual rows if they want to know how their time for the day breaks down, so no quicker than what they have to do already. The second one would be extremely unacceptable since time tracking is the sole reason we are paying for pro level. We are of the opinion that a date filter is such a basic requirement in the context of time tracking that we are surprised it doesn’t exist.

posts.author: liambailey (Liam Bailey)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey there, my team actually created a custom coded solution in this area. It’s a board view that allows you to choose time tracking columns on multiple boards to pull excel reports from where the time is grouped by person/board. Maybe it’ll help some teams out suffering in this area. It’s a private app and just a prototype for now but you can install it with this link. Let me know if you find it useful.
